tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Almost all fail-to-pass tests (test_anchor_link_interface_has_targetOffset, test_registerLink_signature_updated, test_scrollTo_signature_updated, test_linkTargetOffsetRef_exists, etc.) use regex pattern matching on source file contents via open(path).read(). They do not import, call, or execute the code. The pass-to-pass tests do run commands (lint, tsc, jest), but every f2p test is purely text-matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: Since f2p tests only check for text patterns in source files (e.g., regex matching 'targetOffset?: number' in an interface, or 'linkTargetOffsetRef' declaration), a stub that merely adds the right text patterns without functional implementation would pass. For example, adding dead code with the right variable names and signatures would satisfy all f2p regex tests.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All eight behavioral requirements in instruction.md (interface prop, context signatures, offset tracking, registration, precedence, active link detection, cleanup, click handler) have corresponding test functions that check for them.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive function names (e.g., test_anchor_link_interface_has_targetOffset, test_registerLink_stores_targetOffset), have docstrings indicating fail-to-pass vs pass-to-pass, and are logically grouped by feature area.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves modifying React component code, not producing structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, commands, and type signatures in instruction.md are consistent and correct with no apparent typos.
